perturbation.original_text: 9. All Proprietary Information furnished by Health Card to Ciufo is considered loaned for use solely in connection with the Project, and shall be returned by Ciufo to Health Card upon request by Health Card. Ciufo shall certify that it has destroyed or returned all copies of the Proprietary Information in its possession.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. All Proprietary Information furnished by Health Card to Ciufo is considered loaned for use solely in connection with the Project, and shall be returned by Ciufo to Health Card upon request by Health Card. Ciufo may, at its discretion, retain copies of the Proprietary Information for archival purposes, without any certification requirement regarding destruction or return.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction regarding the return and destruction of Proprietary Information. The original text mandates return upon request and certification of destruction/return of all copies. The modified text now allows Ciufo to retain copies for archival purposes and eliminates the certification requirement, which contradicts the original return policy. This affects enforceability because Health Card no longer has a guarantee that the information is not being used or disclosed after the project.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Proprietary Information furnished by Health Card to Ciufo is considered loaned for use solely in connection with the Project, and shall be returned by Ciufo to Health Card upon request by Health Card. Ciufo may, at its discretion, retain copies of the Proprietary Information for archival purposes, without any certification requirement regarding destruction or return.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original clause by allowing Ciufo to retain copies of proprietary information for archival purposes and removes the certification requirement. This directly contradicts the original clause which mandates the return of all proprietary information upon request and certification of destruction/return. The contradicted text is an exact copy of the changed text which creates a conflict because if it were the orignal clause, that clause would have caused the contradiction itself, making Health Card no longer guaranteed that the information is not being used or disclosed after the project, based on the given explanation.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Ciufo (a) must receive and hold the Proprietary Information in trust and in strictest confidence; (b) must protect the Proprietary Information from disclosure and in no event take any action causing, or failing to take reasonable action necessary in order to prevent, any Proprietary Information disclosed to Ciufo to lose its character as Proprietary Information; and (c) must not use, duplicate, reproduce, distribute, disclose or otherwise disseminate the Proprietary Information except to perform the Project. Any and all reproductions of the Proprietary Information must prominently contain a confidentiality legend.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Ciufo (a) must receive and hold the Proprietary Information with reasonable care; (b) must protect the Proprietary Information from intentional disclosure; and (c) may use, duplicate, reproduce, distribute, disclose or otherwise disseminate the Proprietary Information as needed. Any reproductions of the Proprietary Information must contain a confidentiality legend if deemed necessary by Ciufo.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires Ciufo to hold Proprietary Information in strictest confidence, protect it from any disclosure, and only use it for the Project. The modified version now allows for dissemination as needed and lowers the standard of care. The change creates an in-text contradiction by diluting the confidentiality obligation and introducing the possibility of broad dissemination, which previously was not permitted. The change creates uncertainty in enforceability because Health Card no longer has certainty the proprietary information will be protected with the same degree.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ciufo (a) must receive and hold the Proprietary Information with reasonable care; (b) must protect the Proprietary Information from intentional disclosure; and (c) may use, duplicate, reproduce, distribute, disclose or otherwise disseminate the Proprietary Information as needed. Any reproductions of the Proprietary Information must contain a confidentiality legend if deemed necessary by Ciufo.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates 'strictest confidence' and prohibits dissemination of Proprietary Information except for the project. The changed text allows dissemination 'as needed' and lowers the standard of care. This directly contradicts the original intention of strict confidentiality and limited use, creating a strong in-text contradiction within the document and with the contradicted text as it permits for the proprietary information to be disseminated more freely.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Disclosures of the Proprietary Information may be made only to employees, agents, associates or independent contractors of Ciufo (a) who are directly involved in the Project and have a specific need to know such information; and (b) whom Ciufo has obligated to hold the Proprietary Information in trust and in strictest confidence.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Disclosures of the Proprietary Information may be made to any individual or entity at Ciufo's discretion, without any requirement of a specific need to know or obligation to maintain confidentiality.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause limits disclosures of Proprietary Information to those directly involved in the project and bound by confidentiality. The modified clause removes these restrictions, allowing disclosure to anyone at Ciufo's discretion, which is an in-text contradiction to the original intended use of the information, and thus contradicts the original clause by removing these limitations. This creates uncertainty in enforceability because Health Card no longer has certainty the proprietary information will be protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclosures of the Proprietary Information may be made to any individual or entity at Ciufo's discretion, without any requirement of a specific need to know or obligation to maintain confidentiality.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts disclosures of Proprietary Information, while the changed text removes these restrictions, allowing disclosures to anyone at Ciufo's discretion. This directly contradicts the intended use and protection of the information outlined in the original clause, and it directly mirrors and contradicts the text in the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Ciufo acknowledges and agrees that, in the event of any breach or threatened breach of any provision of this agreement, Health Card may be without an adequate remedy at law and, accordingly, shall be entitled to enforce such provision by temporary or permanent injunctive or mandatory relief obtained in an action or proceeding instituted in any court of competent jurisdiction without the necessity of proving damages and without prejudice to any other rights or remedies which it may have at law or in equity. Ciufo hereby agrees to the jurisdiction of all federal and state courts within the state of the defending party in connection with any matter relating to this Agreement. Ciufo further agrees that service of process may be made, in addition to all other methods permitted by law, by Certified Mail, Return Receipt Requested, sent to the address set forth below.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Ciufo acknowledges and agrees that, in the event of any breach or threatened breach of any provision of this agreement, the parties shall first attempt to resolve the dispute through mediation. If mediation fails, Health Card's sole remedy shall be limited to monetary damages, not to exceed $1000. Ciufo does not agree to the jurisdiction of any courts, but rather disputes will be handled by an arbitrator in Wisconsin. Ciufo further agrees that service of process may only be made in person.
perturbation.explanation: This modification severely restricts Health Card's remedies in case of a breach. The original clause allowed for injunctive relief and specified court jurisdiction. The modified clause mandates mediation, limits damages to $1000, and changes the jurisdiction for disputes, contradicting the original enforcement terms. Specifically, limiting the remedies to just 1000$ contradicts the ability for Health Card to receive an adequate remedy at law. This in-text contradiction introduces uncertainty in enforceability and significantly weakens Health Card's position.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ciufo acknowledges and agrees that, in the event of any breach or threatened breach of any provision of this agreement, the parties shall first attempt to resolve the dispute through mediation. If mediation fails, Health Card's sole remedy shall be limited to monetary damages, not to exceed $1000. Ciufo does not agree to the jurisdiction of any courts, but rather disputes will be handled by an arbitrator in Wisconsin. Ciufo further agrees that service of process may only be made in person.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the remedies available to Health Card in case of a breach. The original text allows for injunctive relief and specifies court jurisdiction. The changed text mandates mediation, limits damages to $1000, and changes the jurisdiction for disputes, directly contradicting the original enforcement terms. As the explanation states, limiting the remedies to just 1000$ contradicts the ability for Health Card to receive an adequate remedy at law. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, which means that the changed text is introducing a contradiction with itself, creating an in-text contradiction, aligning with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1. a. "Proprietary Information" means information including, but not limited to clients, pricing and information which is related to the business of Health Card from which Health Card (1) derives economic value, actual or potential, from not being generally known to other persons who can obtain economic value from its disclosure or use; and (2) is the subject of efforts by Health Card that are reasonable under the circumstances to maintain its secrecy, including without limitation (i) with respect to information which has been reduced to tangible form, marking such information clearly and conspicuously with a legend identifying its confidential or proprietary nature; (ii) with respect to any oral presentation or communication, denominating such information as confidential immediately before, during or after such oral presentation or communication; or (iii) otherwise treating such information as confidential. Assuming the criteria in clauses (1) and (2) above are met, Proprietary Information includes, but is not limited to technical and nontechnical information and data related to the formulas, patterns, designs, compilations, programs, inventions, methods, techniques, drawings, processes, finances, actual or potential customers and suppliers, research, development, existing and future products, and employees of Health Card. Proprietary Information also includes information which has been disclosed to Health Card by a third party, which Health Card is obligated to treat as confidential.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. a. "Proprietary Information" means information including, but not limited to clients, pricing and information which is related to the business of Health Card from which Health Card (1) derives economic value, actual or potential, from not being generally known to other persons who can obtain economic value from its disclosure or use; and (2) is the subject of reasonable efforts by Ciufo that are reasonable under the circumstances to maintain its secrecy. Proprietary Information includes only the information explicitly marked as 'Confidential' by Health Card and excludes all other data, regardless of its nature.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of Proprietary Information focuses on Health Card's efforts to maintain secrecy. The modified version shifts the responsibility for maintaining secrecy to Ciufo, and significantly narrows the scope of what qualifies as Proprietary Information to only explicitly marked information. This creates an in-text contradiction because the original clause stated how the Health Card was responsible for making reasonable efforts to keep the information secret, now it says that the responsiblity is of Ciufo. This weakens the protection for Health Card's information, as it's no longer comprehensive and depends on Ciufo's efforts. This change introduces uncertainty in enforceability, because the parties are now in disagreement on who has what kind of responsibility.
perturbation.contradicted_text: a. "Proprietary Information" means information including, but not limited to clients, pricing and information which is related to the business of Health Card from which Health Card (1) derives economic value, actual or potential, from not being generally known to other persons who can obtain economic value from its disclosure or use; and (2) is the subject of reasonable efforts by Ciufo that are reasonable under the circumstances to maintain its secrecy. Proprietary Information includes only the information explicitly marked as 'Confidential' by Health Card and excludes all other data, regardless of its nature.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the definition of 'Proprietary Information'. The original text focuses on Health Card's efforts to maintain secrecy, while the changed text shifts the responsibility to Ciufo and limits Proprietary Information to explicitly marked data. This contradicts the original intention and introduces uncertainty, as Health Card's data protection is now dependent on Ciufo's actions and marking practices. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text in this case, highlights this contradiction by showcasing the new definition's restrictive scope and shifted responsibility, thus resulting in an in-text contradiction.
